gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/176)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/176)
text: كَانَ بِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) وَجَعُ الْبَطْنِ فَأَمَرَ أَنْ يُطْبَخَ لَهُ الْأَرُزُّ وَ يُجْعَلَ عَلَيْهِ السُّمَّاقُ فَأَكَلَهُ فَبَرَأَ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Sa’alba Bin Maymoun, from Humran who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was with stomach pain, so he<sup>asws</sup> instructed that the rice be cooked for him<sup>asws</sup>, and the Sumac (a kind of pepper) sprinkled upon it. So he<sup>asws</sup> ate and was cured’.
en.sarwar: 7. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn Faddal from Tha’labah ibn Maymun from Humran who has said the following: “Abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once experienced pain in his belly and he ordered to cook rice for him (the Imam) with sumac on it, and after eating it the pain went away from him (the Imam).’”
